serviceDescription.aws: Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) provides a simple, serverless, set-and-forget elastic file system for use with AWS Cloud services and on-premises resources.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) provides a simple, serverless, set-and-forget elastic file system for use with Amazon Web Services Cloud services and on-premises resources.
serviceDescription.aws-us-gov: Amazon Elastic File System (EFS) provides a simple, serverless, set-and-forget elastic file system for use with AWS Cloud services and on-premises resources.

templates.title: Standard Storage
templates.description: Performance-optimized, highly available, and durable storage classes within a region, providing continuous availability in the event that an AZ is unavailable. The Standard-Infrequent Access storage class is cost-optimized for files accessed less frequently and costs 92% less than Standard storage.

cards.title: Throughput Mode Selector
cards.helpText: You can select Provisioned Throughput or Elastic Throughput modes. Elastic Throughput lets you use as much throughput as your workload needs, if you have unpredictable and spiky application traffic. With Elastic Throughput, you only pay for the amount of data that you read and write to your file system. Provisioned Throughput lets you configure file system throughput independent of the amount of data stored and pay separately for storage and throughput provisioned. Like the default Bursting Throughput mode, the Provisioned Throughput mode also includes 50 KB/s per GB (or 1 MB/s per 20 GB) of throughput in

components.label: Choose the throughput mode of your file system

cards.title: Storage settings
cards.helpText: With Amazon EFS, you pay only for the resources that you use. There is no minimum fee and there are no set-up charges. There are four storage classes for Amazon EFS: two regional storage classes, Standard storage class and Standard Infrequent Access (EFS Standard-IA) storage class, and two single Availability Zone storage classes, One Zone and One Zone-Infrequent Access (EFS One Zone-IA). The Standard and One Zone storage classes are designed for active file system workloads and the Standard Infrequent Access and One Zone Infrequent-Access storage classed are cost-optimized for files accessed less frequently. Customers can use infrequent access storage classes by creating a new file system and enabling Lifecycle Management. With Lifecycle Management enabled, EFS automatically will move files that have not been accessed for 30 days from the Standard or One Zone storage class to the Standard-Infrequent Access or One Zone-Infrequent Access storage class.

components.label: Desired Storage Capacity
components.description: Enter the amount of EFS storage capacity you expect to use. EFS is an elastic file system that grows and shrinks based on actual usage and you pay only for what you use. We recommend you input your average usage for the month.

components.text:
Pecentage of data infrequently used
Industry research and customer analysis shows that on average, 5% of files are frequently accessed, 15% are accessed a few times within months, and 80% are accessed once a year. Enter 0 or leave blank if you do not intend to use Lifecycle Management policies to cost optimize your EFS file system.

components.label: Infrequent Access storage - Percentage of data that is accessed multiple times within a few months
Archive storage - Percentage of data that is accessed once a year

components.header: Total Percentage of Infrequent Access Storage and Archive Storage can't be greater than 100%
components.label: Percent alert

triggerComponent.variableLabel: Total Percentage of Data in IA and AA Storage

components.text:
Infrequent Access and Archive requests
You pay for the transitions between storage classes, when the policies to move files to and from the Infrequent Access and Archive storage classes are set (EFS Intelligent-Tiering). Alternately, you pay a fee every time you directly access files from the infrequent access storage. Enter the amount of data in infrequent access storage that you expect to access per month.

cards.title: Throughput
cards.helpText: You can select Provisioned Throughput or Elastic Throughput modes. Elastic Throughput lets you use as much throughput as your workload needs, if you have unpredictable and spiky application traffic. With Elastic Throughput, you only pay for the amount of data that you read and write to your file system. Provisioned Throughput lets you configure file system throughput independent of the amount of data stored and pay separately for storage and throughput provisioned. Like the default Bursting Throughput mode, the Provisioned Throughput mode also includes 50 KB/s per GB (or 1 MB/s per 20 GB) of throughput in the price of storage. You are billed only for the throughput provisioned above what you are provided based on data you have stored.

components.label: Provisioned Throughput
components.description: Enter the throughput amount that you want in addition to the throughput provided based on the size of your file system.

templates.title: One Zone Storage
templates.description: Low-cost, performance-optimized, highly available, and durable storage classes within an AZ. The One Zone–Infrequent Access storage class is cost-optimized for files accessed less frequently and costs 92% less than One Zone storage.

cards.title: Storage settings
cards.helpText: EFS One Zone Storage classes offer customers the choice of additional savings by choosing to store their data in a single-AZ. The One Zone Storage Class is designed for active file system workloads and you pay only for the amount of file system storage you use per month. The One Zone- Infrequent Access storage class is cost-optimized for files accessed less frequently. Data stored on the One Zone-Infrequent Access storage class costs 92% less than One Zone storage, and you pay an Infrequent Access Request fee each time you read from or write to a file.

components.label: Desired Storage Capacity
components.description: Enter the amount of EFS storage capacity you expect to use. EFS is an elastic file system that grows and shrinks based on actual usage and you pay only for what you use. We recommend you input your average usage for the month.

components.label: Percentage of data that is frequently accessed
components.description: Industry research and customer analysis shows that on average, 20% of files are frequently accessed, and 80% are infrequently accessed. Enter 100 or leave blank if you do not intend to use Lifecycle Management policies to cost optimize your EFS file system.

components.label: Infrequent Access requests
components.description: You pay for the transitions between storage classes, when the policies to move files to and from the infrequent access storage classes are set (EFS Intelligent-Tiering). Alternately, you pay a fee every time you directly access files from the infrequent access storage. Enter the amount of data in infrequent access storage that you expect to access per month.

cards.title: Throughput
cards.helpText: You can optionally select the Provisioned Throughput mode and provision the throughput of your file system independent of the amount of data stored and pay separately for storage and throughput. Like the default Bursting Throughput mode, the Provisioned Throughput mode also includes 50 KB/s per GB (or 1 MB/s per 20 GB) of throughput in the price of storage. You are billed only for the throughput provisioned above what you are provided based on data you have stored.
